sessions.intent: Design the Callback feature: state-machine restoration via seek + bind, two issuers (ask-user, error-retry) sharing one resume primitive.

before.assumptions: Callstack work is merged on runtime2. Diff infrastructure exists per Call. ISnapshotted does not yet exist. No callback-related code in App/. The conversation drove the design — no prior plan or review feedback to respond to.
before.risk: Misframing Callback as event-sourcing-style replay would force determinism contracts the language doesn't have. Conflating ask-user and error-retry capture policies under one annotation would over-burden every step. Allowing cross-island ref-capture would couple ISnapshotted types and reinvent BinaryFormatter.

actions.context: Captures the settled design from a long architect-mode whiteboard with Ingi: seek-and-bind primitive, two issuers with different capture policies, per-type ISnapshotted, three buckets, islands rule (values only, ref-capture rejected), throw-time-vars-via-diff-reverse-apply, Diff auto-flip on error, %!error.callback% surface, smallest-first-cut test, subsystem inventory first-pass, seven open threads deferred to future sessions.
Version summary — what was decided, key rejections, what's next.

after.status: design v1 written; no code changes; ready for subsystem walk in next architect session
after.health: green — design is internally consistent and Ingi confirmed each fork in turn
after.notes: Doc captures all conversation context so a future session can pick up without rediscovery. Seven open threads enumerated, none blocking the smallest-first-cut. Recommend test-designer next once we agree the design is final, or another architect session to walk subsystems type-by-type.
